content.text: typeof and instanceof are JavaScript's two built-in runtime type-inspection operators. TypeScript integrates them as type guards: after a typeof or instanceof check, the compiler narrows the variable to the type that passed the test.

attrs.alt: typeof return values table and instanceof prototype chain diagram showing HttpError extends Error extends Object

content.text: typeof returns one of eight string literals. Critically, typeof null === "object" — a historical JavaScript quirk. Checking for an object shape always requires a null guard.
typeof with unknown — Validating External Data
Null Guard — The Essential typeof Companion
instanceof checks whether the prototype chain of a value contains the prototype of the given constructor. It returns true for the class itself and all ancestors in the prototype chain.
Error Subclass Narrowing — The Most Common instanceof Use
string, number, boolean, undefined, bigint, symbol, function

When a function parameter is a union of primitive types, using typeof checks in a switch or if-else chain enables exhaustive narrowing. TypeScript tracks which branches have been covered and reports the type of any unhandled members.
When a class hierarchy shares a base type, instanceof is the most direct way to call subclass-specific methods while keeping the variable typed at the base level.
typeof with Environment Variables in Angular
